chunk_summary: This section outlines discharge criteria and post-discharge follow-up responsibilities for children with complicated severe acute malnutrition (SAM), emphasizing weight gain, medical stability, caregiver education, and community-based monitoring to prevent relapse.
learning_objectives: Understand the discharge criteria for children with complicated SAM, including weight gain and medical stability.
Learn the importance of caregiver counseling and community-based follow-up to prevent relapse.
Identify post-discharge monitoring protocols, including nutritional rehabilitation and infection prevention.
Recognize the role of immunization updates and micronutrient supplementation in SAM management.
key_facts: Discharge requires >15% weight gain from admission, no edema, and satisfactory weight gain (>5 g/kg/day for 3 consecutive days).
Transfer to community care is possible with good appetite, no edema, and completed antibiotic course.
Post-discharge follow-up includes weight and edema monitoring, micronutrient supplementation, and infection screening in the first week.
Weeks 2-4 focus on relapse prevention, deworming, and WASH behaviors to reduce infections.
Immunizations must be updated before discharge to ensure comprehensive care.
reference_quote: Operational guidance commonly requires: >15% weight gain from admission and no signs of acute illness; edema resolved; satisfactory weight gain for 3 consecutive days (often >5 g/kg/day).
